output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory backend for managing a collaborative team workspace:
users, projects, tasks, messaging (with attachments and threaded replies),
performance dashboards, peer ratings and exportable reports (CSV / PDF).

Standard library only, ASCII only.

Design notes
------------
* A single re-entrant lock (``RLock``) is shared by the manager and every
  sub-store so that compound, cross-store operations (cascading deletes,
  index maintenance, id allocation) are atomic and consistent under
  concurrent access.
* All read accessors return deep copies so callers can never mutate the
  internal store by mutating a returned object or list.
* Ids are allocated from a single monotonic counter starting at 1 and are
  therefore globally unique across every entity type.
"""

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role of a user within the workspace."""

    ADMIN = "admin"
    MANAGER = "manager"
    MEMBER = "member"
    VIEWER = "viewer"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle status of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kinds of report the manager can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for all errors raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input data fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int


@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    data: bytes = b""


@dataclass
class Message:
    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Collection:
    """A lightweight keyed store that shares the manager's lock.

    The shared lock makes cross-collection operations atomic and lets tests
    assert that every store participates in the same critical section.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, object] = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Coordinates users, projects, tasks, messaging and reporting."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()

        # Sub-stores share the single manager lock.
        self.users = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Collection(self._lock)  # holds Rating objects
        self.reports = _Collection(self._lock)       # reserved for report caching

        # Secondary indexes (manager owned, all guarded by self._lock).
        self._username_to_id: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._email_to_id: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._assignee_index: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._project_tasks: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._project_messages: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._task_messages: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._ratee_ratings: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}

        self._id_counter = 0

    # -- id allocation ----------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # -- internal lookups (assume lock held) ------------------------------
    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message  # type: ignore[return-value]

    # ===================================================================
    # User management
    # ===================================================================
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            self._validate_username(username)
            self._validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self._username_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self._email_to_id:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user_id = self._next_id()
            user = User(user_id, username, email, role, self._now())
            self.users._items[user_id] = user
            self._username_to_id[username] = user_id
            self._email_to_id[email] = user_id
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                copy.deepcopy(self.users._items[uid])
                for uid in sorted(self.users._items)
            ]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                self._validate_username(username)
                if username != user.username and username in self._username_to_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email is not None:
                self._validate_email(email)
                if email != user.email and email in self._email_to_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")
            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None and username != user.username:
                self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
                user.username = username
                self._username_to_id[username] = user_id
            if email is not None and email != user.email:
                self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)
                user.email = email
                self._email_to_id[email] = user_id
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise ConflictError("user owns one or more projects")

            assigned = self._assignee_index.get(user_id, [])
            for task_id in assigned:
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            # Detach any remaining (completed) tasks from the user.
            for task_id in list(assigned):
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None:
                    task.assignee_id = None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            self._assignee_index.pop(user_id, None)

            del self.users._items[user_id]
            self._username_to_id.pop(user.username, None)
            self._email_to_id.pop(user.email, None)

    # ===================================================================
    # Project management
    # ===================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            self._validate_name(name, "project name")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)

            project_id = self._next_id()
            project = Project(
                project_id, name, description or "", start_date, end_date, owner_id
            )
            self.projects._items[project_id] = project
            self._project_tasks.setdefault(project_id, [])
            self._project_messages.setdefault(project_id, [])
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                copy.deepcopy(self.projects._items[pid])
                for pid in sorted(self.projects._items)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            if name is not None:
                self._validate_name(name, "project name")
            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is None else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is None else end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self._project_tasks.get(project_id, [])):
                self._remove_task_internal(task_id)
            for message_id in list(self._project_messages.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)

            self._project_messages.pop(project_id, None)
            self._project_tasks.pop(project_id, None)
            del self.projects._items[project_id]

    # ===================================================================
    # Task management
    # ===================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            self._validate_name(title, "task title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")

            task_id = self._next_id()
            task = Task(
                id=task_id,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task_id] = task
            self._project_tasks.setdefault(project_id, []).append(task_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._assignee_index.setdefault(assignee_id, []).append(task_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._project_tasks.get(project_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.tasks._items[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self._assignee_index.get(user_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.tasks._items[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, new_assignee_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, None)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            elif status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    # -- task helpers (assume lock held) ----------------------------------
    def _set_assignee(self, task: Task, new_assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        old = task.assignee_id
        if old == new_assignee_id:
            return
        if old is not None:
            bucket = self._assignee_index.get(old)
            if bucket and task.id in bucket:
                bucket.remove(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
        if new_assignee_id is not None:
            self._assignee_index.setdefault(new_assignee_id, []).append(task.id)

    def _remove_task_internal(self, task_id: int) -> None:
        task = self.tasks._items.pop(task_id, None)
        if task is None:
            return
        if task.assignee_id is not None:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            bucket = self._assignee_index.get(task.assignee_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            if bucket and task_id in bucket:
                bucket.remove(task_id)
        plist = self._project_tasks.get(task.project_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        if plist and task_id in plist:
            plist.remove(task_id)
        for message_id in list(self._task_messages.get(task_id, [])):
            self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
        self._task_messages.pop(task_id, None)

    # ===================================================================
    # Messaging
    # ===================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            atts = attachments or []
            if not self._has_text(body) and not atts:
                raise ValidationError("message must have a body or attachments")

            now = self._now()
            attachment_objs = [
                self._build_attachment(filename, data, author_id, now)
                for filename, data in atts
            ]

            message_id = self._next_id()
            message = Message(
                id=message_id,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=attachment_objs,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message_id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._project_messages.setdefault(project_id, []).append(message_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._task_messages.setdefault(task_id, []).append(message_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not self._has_text(new_body) and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message must have a body or attachments")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may add attachments")
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename is required")
            attachment = self._build_attachment(
                filename, data, uploader_id, self._now()
            )
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._project_messages.get(project_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.messages._items[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self._task_messages.get(task_id, []))
            return [copy.deepcopy(self.messages._items[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
            if message.project_id is not None:
                bucket = self._project_messages.get(message.project_id)
                if bucket and message_id in bucket:
                    bucket.remove(message_id)
            if message.task_id is not None:
                bucket = self._task_messages.get(message.task_id)
                if bucket and message_id in bucket:
                    bucket.remove(message_id)

    # -- message helpers --------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _build_attachment(
        filename: str, data: bytes, uploaded_by: int, uploaded_at: datetime
    ) -> Attachment:
        payload = bytes(data) if data is not None else b""
        return Attachment(
            filename=filename,
            size=len(payload),
            uploaded_by=uploaded_by,
            uploaded_at=uploaded_at,
            data=payload,
        )

    # ===================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ===================================================================
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_user(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating_id = self._next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rating_id,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating_id] = rating
            self._ratee_ratings.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ratee_ratings.get(user_id, []))
            ratings = [self.performance._items[rid] for rid in ids]
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [copy.deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_user(user_id)
            metrics = self._task_metrics(tasks)
            ratings = self._ratings_aggregate(user_id)
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": metrics["total"],
                "completed_tasks": metrics["completed"],
                "in_progress_tasks": metrics["in_progress"],
                "not_started_tasks": metrics["not_started"],
                "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": metrics["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": ratings["count"],
                "rating_average": ratings["average"],
            }

    # -- performance helpers (assume lock held) ---------------------------
    def _tasks_for_user(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        ids = self._assignee_index.get(user_id, [])
        return [self.tasks._items[tid] for tid in ids if tid in self.tasks._items]  # type: ignore[misc]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_durations(tasks: List[Task]) -> List[float]:
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                durations.append((task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds())
        return durations

    def _task_metrics(self, tasks: List[Task]) -> Dict[str, object]:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
        rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
        durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
        avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        return {
            "total": total,
            "completed": completed,
            "in_progress": in_progress,
            "not_started": not_started,
            "completion_rate": rate,
            "average_completion_time": avg_time,
        }

    def _ratings_aggregate(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        ids = self._ratee_ratings.get(user_id, [])
        ratings = [self.performance._items[rid] for rid in ids if rid in self.performance._items]
        count = len(ratings)
        average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        return {"count": count, "average": average}

    # ===================================================================
    # Reports
    # ===================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._project_tasks.get(project_id, []))
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[tid] for tid in ids]
            now = self._now()

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

            total = len(tasks)
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.deadline < now
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )

            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, int]] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    continue
                bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "in_progress": 0, "not_started": 0},
                )
                bucket["total"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    bucket["completed"] += 1
                elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                    bucket["in_progress"] += 1
                else:
                    bucket["not_started"] += 1

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                task_ids = self._project_tasks.get(project_id, [])
                scoped_tasks = [self.tasks._items[tid] for tid in task_ids]
                grouped: Dict[int, List[Task]] = {}
                for task in scoped_tasks:
                    if task.assignee_id is not None:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                        grouped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                user_ids = sorted(grouped)
            else:
                grouped = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
                user_ids = sorted(self.users._items)

            rows = []
            for uid in user_ids:
                if grouped is not None:
                    tasks = grouped.get(uid, [])
                else:
                    tasks = self._tasks_for_user(uid)
                metrics = self._task_metrics(tasks)
                ratings = self._ratings_aggregate(uid)
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": uid,
                        "total_tasks": metrics["total"],
                        "completed_tasks": metrics["completed"],
                        "in_progress_tasks": metrics["in_progress"],
                        "not_started_tasks": metrics["not_started"],
                        "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                        "average_completion_time": metrics["average_completion_time"],
                        "rating_count": ratings["count"],
                        "rating_average": ratings["average"],
                    }
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self._assignee_index.get(user_id, []))
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[tid] for tid in ids]
            metrics = self._task_metrics(tasks)
            ratings = self._ratings_aggregate(user_id)
            task_rows = [
                {
                    "id": t.id,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    "title": t.title,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    "status": t.status.value,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    "deadline": t.deadline,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    "started_at": t.started_at,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": metrics["total"],
                "completed_tasks": metrics["completed"],
                "in_progress_tasks": metrics["in_progress"],
                "not_started_tasks": metrics["not_started"],
                "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": metrics["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": ratings["count"],
                "rating_average": ratings["average"],
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    def export_report(self, report: object, report_format: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    # -- report serialization ---------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_csv(report: Dict[str, object]) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "in_progress_tasks",
                "not_started_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])
        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(["task_id", "title", "status", "started_at", "completed_at"])
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                writer.writerow(
                    [
                        task.get("id", ""),
                        task.get("title", ""),
                        task.get("status", ""),
                        task.get("started_at", ""),
                        task.get("completed_at", ""),
                    ]
                )
        elif report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", report.get("project_id", "")])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks", 0)])
            writer.writerow(["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate", 0.0)])
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks", 0)])
            for status, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status_" + str(status), count])
        else:
            writer.writerow(["key", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, value])

        return buffer.getvalue()

    def _report_to_pdf(self, report: Dict[str, object]) -> bytes:
        return self._build_pdf(self._report_text_lines(report))

    @staticmethod
    def _report_text_lines(report: Dict[str, object]) -> List[str]:
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, dict):
                lines.append("{0}:".format(key))
                for sub_key, sub_value in value.items():
                    lines.append("  {0}: {1}".format(sub_key, sub_value))
            elif isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} item(s)".format(key, len(value)))
                for item in value[:100]:
                    lines.append("  - {0}".format(item))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(text_lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
        """Build a minimal, valid single-page PDF document."""

        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            text = text.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)")
            return "".join(ch for ch in text if 32 <= ord(ch) < 127)

        stream_lines = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "14 TL", "1 0 0 1 50 760 Tm"]
        rendered = text_lines if text_lines else [""]
        for index, line in enumerate(rendered):
            if index == 0:
                stream_lines.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(line)))
            else:
                stream_lines.append("T*")
                stream_lines.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(line)))
        stream_lines.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(stream_lines).encode("ascii", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(stream)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + stream
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        out = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(out))
            out += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("ascii")
            out += body
            out += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_pos = len(out)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        out += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
        out += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            out += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        out += b"trailer\n"
        out += "<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
        out += b"startxref\n"
        out += "{0}\n".format(xref_pos).encode("ascii")
        out += b"%%EOF\n"
        return bytes(out)

    # ===================================================================
    # Validation helpers
    # ===================================================================
    @staticmethod
    def _has_text(value: object) -> bool:
        return isinstance(value, str) and bool(value.strip())

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_username(username: object) -> None:
        if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
            raise ValidationError("username is required")

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email: object) -> None:
        if not isinstance(email, str) or not email.strip() or "@" not in email:
            raise ValidationError("a valid email is required")

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_name(name: object, label: str) -> None:
        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
            raise ValidationError("{0} is required".format(label))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - simple smoke demonstration
    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager()
    alice = mgr.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    bob = mgr.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com")
    project = mgr.create_project(
        "Launch", datetime(2024, 1, 1), datetime(2024, 12, 31), alice.id
    )
    task = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Write spec", assignee_id=bob.id)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    print("completion rate:", mgr.get_completion_rate(bob.id))
    report = mgr.team_performance_report()
    print(mgr.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))
